title: Symponik Nabooalla
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}}
{{InUniverseMedia
|image=[[File:Palace_Plaza.png]]
|name="Symponik Nabooalla"
|author=[[Augara Jowil/Legends|Augara Jowil]]<ref name="DB">{{DB|character|augie|Jowil, Augara "Augie"}}</ref>
|editor=
|illustrator=

"'''Symponik Nabooalla'''" was a [[symponik]] composed by the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Augara Jowil/Legends|Augara Jowil]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. It commemorated the meeting of Gungan [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Rugor Nass]] and [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]] [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] under the circumstances of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion]] of their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. The first movement of the piece was performed by the [[Great Municipal Band/Legends|Great Municipal Band]] during the victory [[Parade/Legends|parade]] in the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Theed/Legends|Theed]] after the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] was freed from the Federation.

==Description==
The "Symponik Nabooalla" was a [[symponik]], a [[Music/Legends|musical]] epic meant to retell a significant event in [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] history. The symponik was written to record the story of the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Naboo (people)/Legends|inhabitants]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] coming before the Gungan [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Rugor Nass/Legends|Rugor Nass]] to ask for assistance in freeing their world from the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invading]] [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]].<ref name="DB" /> The composition included parts for [[bawoonka]]s, [[Drum/Legends|drums]],<ref name="FF108" /> and vocalists. The first movement of the piece contained melodies utilizing five different pitches, and it ended with a large crescendo as the [[Horn/Legends|horns]] played phrases that grew higher and higher.<ref name="TPMS" />

==History==
[[File:Great_Municipal_Band.png|thumb|left|250px|The Great Municipal Band performing the "Symponik Nabooalla"]]
The piece was composed by Gungan [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[musikagung]] [[Augara Jowil/Legends|Augara "Augie" Jowil]], leader of the Gungan [[Great Municipal Band/Legends|Great Municipal Band]], on the planet Naboo in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. Jowil composed the entire piece in her [[Brain/Legends|head]]<ref name="DB" /> in order to commemorate the combined efforts of Humans and Gungans to restore freedom to their planet from the invading Trade Federation, as she saw that as a turning point in Gungan history.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|175|Jowil, Augarra "Augie"}}</ref> It more specifically celebrated the meeting of Gungan Boss Rugor Nass and Naboo [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]]<ref name="DB" /> when the Human Naboo people, as part of a plan to end the global invasion by the Federation, humbly beseeched the Gungans for aid.<ref name="TPM">{{Film|I}}</ref>

The first movement of the musical piece debuted in the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Theed/Legends|Theed]] during a Great Municipal [[Band/Legends|Band]] performance in the victory [[Parade/Legends|parade]] after the defeat of the Trade Federation.<ref name="DB" /> The movement was performed on bawoonkas and drums<ref name="FF108">{{FactFile|108|VIC 1-4|Victory on Naboo}}</ref> and was sung by a [[choir]]. It was played before a crowd of Humans, Gungans, and representatives of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], including [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]].<ref name="TPM" /> The composition concreted Jowil's place in Gungan history as one of the greatest [[Musician/Legends|conductors]] of the Municipal Band of all [[Time/Legends|time]].<ref name="DB" />

==Excerpt==
{{Listen|filename=Symponik Nabooalla.flac|description=The piece's first movement<ref name="TPMS" />}}

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Most of it was synthesizer. The live stuff was vocal and brass. Some of the brass licks were done at half-tempo and then speeded up so it got to be an octave higher, where normal trumpets can't go.|Ken Wannberg|<ref name="ST.COM">{{WebCite|url=http://www.soundtrackmag.com/kwannberg.html|text=Music Editor Ken Wannburg|author=Thaxton, Ford A.|work=Soundtrackmag.com|archivedate=200101290440}}</ref>}}
[[File:AugiesGreatMunicipalBand.jpg|thumb|right|The piano sheet music for the piece composed by John Williams]]
The "Symponik Nabooalla" made its first appearance in the final scene of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] during the victory parade in Theed.<ref name="TPM" /> The piece was included on the original release of the [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (soundtrack)|''Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace'' soundtrack]] as track seventeen, which is titled "Augie's Great Municipal Band and End Credits," and in the thirty-first track of the Ultimate Edition soundtrack, which is titled "The Parade." It was composed by [[John Williams]], performed by the [[London Symphony Orchestra]] and the [[New London Children's Choir]]<ref name="TPMS">[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (soundtrack)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' soundtrack]]</ref> and recorded in [[Wikipedia:England|England]]. [[Wikipedia:Synthesizer|Synthesizers]] were used for most of the recording. The [[Wikipedia:Singing|vocals]] and [[Wikipedia:Brass instrument|brass]] were live, though certain brass phrases were performed at half-[[Wikipedia:Tempo|tempo]] and sped up in order to reach [[Wikipedia:Note|notes]] an [[Wikipedia:Octave|octave]] out of a [[Wikipedia:Trumpet|trumpet's]] range.<ref name="ST.COM" /> It was written in the [[Wikipedia:A major|key of A]], is [[Wikipedia:Polyphony|polyphonic]], has a tempo of 124 bpm, and has a [[Wikipedia:Meter (music)|duple meter]] with a time signature of 4/4.<ref name="TPMMB">[[Music From Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace'' promotional book]]</ref> A [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry about Jowil was also written by [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] member [[Chad Finke]] through the ''[[What's The Story?]]'' feature on [[StarWars.com]], detailing much about the piece and its origins and naming the [[in-universe]] piece for the first time.<ref name="DB" />

It was originally rumored that the track "Augie's Great Municipal Band" was an intentional reworking of "[[The Emperor's Theme]]," having been sped up and switched to a major key in order to evoke a happy emotion rather than a sinister one.<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/1940421|board=Star Wars And Film Music|thread=Augie's Great Municipal Band - The Debate|user=Grand Moff Joker|date=2001-02-13|time=8:46 AM|archivedate=20151204055725}}</ref> [[Ken Wannberg]], music editor for ''The Phantom Menace'', stated in a Soundtrackmag.com interview that the correlation was in no way intentional, and he went on to say the two pieces didn't even sound alike.<ref name="ST.COM" /> This, however, was conflicted in the ''[[Star Wars Insider 109]]'' article "[[Menace Revisited]]," where it was stated that "Augie's Great Municipal Band" was intentionally reworked from "The Emperor's Theme" to symbolize Palpatine's first step to galactic dominion.<ref name="Menace Revisited">{{InsiderCite|109|Menace Revisited}}</ref> This position was later backed by former [[LucasArts]] lead sound designer [[David W. Collins]] and author [[Timothy Zahn]] in [[Star Wars Insider 147|''Star Wars Insider'' 147]].<ref name="Insider147">{{InsiderCite|147|50 Greatest Reasons to Love the Star Wars Prequels!|50 Greatest Reasons to Love the ''Star Wars'' Prequels!}}</ref>

The game [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (video game)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' video game]] contains the victory parade scene at the end of the last mission, but it utilizes the track "Hail to the Winner, Anakin Skywalker" instead of "Augie's Great Municipal Band."<ref name="TPMVG">[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (video game)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' video game]]</ref> The piece was also used during the credits for the first two installments of the 2001 mockumentary ''[[R2-D2: Beneath the Dome]]'',<ref name="BTD">''[[R2-D2: Beneath the Dome]]''</ref> which began airing following that year's Thanksgiving showing of ''The Phantom Menace'' on [[20th Century Studios|FOX]].<ref name="BTD SW.com">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/release/media/news20011022.html|text=Fox Previews ''R2-D2: Beneath the Dome''}}</ref>
